summary: Correct the long-vector implementation so sparse arrays are invalid and replaced named sets cannot retain caller-owned vector aliases.
specSections: Section 2.7 test-first evidence and milestone gates

decisions.topic: Dense Customizer vectors and imported-set ownership
decisions.decision: Require each numeric vector index from zero through length minus one to be an own finite-number property in both compatibility references and candidate values, and serialize only after the complete dense sequence passes that check. When replacing named sets, create a prototype-safe value record and clone every ParameterValue so vector arrays become state-owned.
decisions.rationale: Array every, some, and map skip missing elements, so a sparse array could pass the finite-number guard and serialize with absent components. Separately, shallow-spreading a named-set value record retained each caller-owned vector, allowing post-reducer mutation to silently change application state and exported stock JSON. Explicit indexed validation and the existing scalar-or-vector clone boundary close both holes without adding a component limit or weakening exact-length, nonempty, finiteness, and prototype-key behavior.

testEvidence.criterion: Vector compatibility and serialization reject missing numeric indices
testEvidence.test: Construct fully sparse and one-own-hole six-element arrays, supply the missing component only through the latter array's prototype, require compatibility rejection and serialization failure for both, then require a sparse reference to reject an otherwise dense same-length candidate while retaining dense long-vector acceptance.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: Test Files 1 failed (1); Tests 2 failed | 2 passed (4). Four assertions showed that fully and partially sparse arrays both returned compatible and both serialized without throwing.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 5 passed (5); Duration 582ms.

testEvidence.criterion: Replaced named sets own their long vectors and retain scalar and prototype-shaped values
testEvidence.test: Replace sets with a caller-owned six-component pose, scalar width, and own __proto__ vector; mutate all caller records after the reducer returns; require stored values and encoded stock JSON to retain the original bytes and the prototype-shaped key as an inert own property.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/parameters/parameter-state.test.ts -t "owns replaced-set vector values"
red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: Test Files 1 failed (1); Tests 1 failed | 9 skipped (10). Mutating the caller changed the stored pose tail and the own __proto__ vector head from their original values to 99.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/parameters/parameter-state.test.ts -t "owns replaced-set vector values"
green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 1 passed | 9 skipped (10); Duration 679ms.

questions: Falsification RED: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/parameters/customizer-schema.test.ts -t "rejects a sparse vector reference" produced Test Files 1 failed (1) and Tests 1 failed | 4 skipped (5), proving a sparse reference also needed indexed validation.
